payload.overview:
[
  {
    "doc_type": "business_context",
    "tenant_id": "tenant_1",
    "version": "v1",
    "payload": {
      "overview": "Vessels, Machinery & Components (Master Data)

This area provides the foundation for all operational data. Vessels represent the ships in your fleet. Machinery and Components capture the real-world hierarchy on each vessel, enabling a clear chain from a ship to its equipment and sub-parts. SFI codes standardize classification so maintenance tasks and parts align with the same vocabulary. This master data is shared and reused by Planned Maintenance, Forms, Documents, Inventory, and Procurement.

Key relationships:
- Vessels hold machinery and component hierarchies used by maintenance planning and inventory compatibility.
- Components are linked to activities through component-to-activity mappings.
- SFI codes provide consistent categorization across maintenance and parts.

Key models: vessel.model.js, vessel.machinery.master.model.js, machinery.model.js, component.model.js, component.activity.model.js, sfi.code.model.js


Planned Maintenance

Plan, assign, execute, and trace all scheduled work on vessels. Schedules define planned tasks; activities are the actionable units your teams perform. As work progresses, the system records work history entries and events (start, pause, resume, complete, add note, attach evidence, etc.). Revisions track any changes to plans over time for auditability.

What it does for you:
- Provides a clear list of due and upcoming activities per vessel and component.
- Captures execution evidence (notes, readings, attachments) and who performed each step, when, and why.
- Maintains revision history so planners and auditors can see how the plan evolved.
- Connects to Inventory so parts consumption is properly recorded against real work.

Important behaviors and relationships:
- Activities belong to schedules and are linked to components and vessels.
- Work histories and their events form a timeline of execution with attachments that are preserved and accessible consistently.
- When parts are issued during work, the system uses Inventory Transactions as the single source of truth, validating location-level stock and rolling back if a work history event is removed.

Key models: maintenance.schedule.model.js, activity.model.js, maintenance.revision.model.js, activity.work.history.model.js, activity.work.history.event.model.js, component.activity.model.js, file.model.js


Forms & Data Capture

Standardize data collection using templates and configurable sequences/cycles. Templates can be mapped to vessels or directly to activities. Forms created from templates support optional/required logic to keep crews focused while ensuring compliance. A full action history records create, submit, validate, or approve steps, and file-backed key–value fields capture documents and images uniformly.

What it does for you:
- Enforces consistent data capture at the right moments (e.g., before/after maintenance, safety checks).
- Supports recurring sequences/cycles for periodic reporting.
- Applies tags and defaults so forms are discoverable and consistently classified.

Important behaviors and relationships:
- Templates can be assigned to vessels and activities; forms then follow the underlying plan.
- Optional forms are clearly marked, and users can commit work when all required forms are complete.
- Tag inheritance helps standardize classification; inherited tags show as non-removable on child forms.
- File-backed form fields (e.g., images, documents) are handled consistently and stored reliably.

Key models: form.template.model.js, form.model.js, form.action.history.model.js, form.sequence.model.js, form.sequence.item.model.js, form.sequence.cycle.model.js, form.template.vessel.mapping.model.js, form.template.activity.mapping.model.js, file.kv.pair.model.js


Document Management

Organize technical manuals, certificates, procedures, and other controlled documents with full version history. Metadata (title, description, status, tags) is cleanly separated from file versions so teams always know the latest approved version while keeping prior revisions for reference. Documents can be archived to prevent changes, and downloads preserve the original filename for consistency.

What it does for you:
- Ensures a single place to find the latest approved documentation.
- Preserves a complete audit trail of prior versions and approvals.
- Applies tags so documents are quickly filtered and discovered.

Important behaviors and relationships:
- The system differentiates document metadata from file binaries for robust versioning.
- Archived documents disable actions to prevent accidental edits.
- Tags and classification support searching across vessels, maintenance, and inventory contexts.

Key models: document.metadata.model.js, document.file.model.js, document.type.model.js, file.model.js, searchable.tag.model.js


Inventory Management

Control spare parts from catalog to bin-level stock. Track what you have, where it is (vessel stores, shore warehouses, workshops), and how it moves (issue, receipt, transfer, adjustment, return). The system validates location-specific quantities and provides a location-wise view for shipboard teams. Suppliers are maintained centrally for consistent purchasing and replenishment.

What it does for you:
- Provides a single source of truth for availability at each location.
- Avoids over-issuing by validating against the specific store being used.
- Connects stock usage back to maintenance work for accurate consumption and costing.

Important behaviors and relationships:
- Parts can be vessel-specific or global, with compatibility to machinery.
- Transactions—issue, receipt, transfer, adjustment, return—record who did what, where, when, and at what cost.
- Virtual Work Site/Crew locations support issuing parts to live work, with full traceability.
- Vessel-level filters ensure shipboard users only see relevant items.

Key models: inventory.part.model.js, inventory.location.model.js, inventory.stock.model.js, inventory.transaction.model.js, vendor.model.js


Procurement

Manage purchasing centrally and enable controlled replenishment. Purchase Orders coordinate buying with vendors, approvals, and receiving. Replenish Orders support requesting stock top-ups within defined roles and processes. Attachments can be stored where needed for traceability.

What it does for you:
- Keeps purchasing and replenishment aligned with real stock and demand.
- Preserves an audit trail from request to receipt.
- Integrates vendors across procurement and inventory.

Important behaviors and relationships:
- Purchase Orders are managed centrally; Replenish Orders enable requests where full purchasing is restricted.
- Receipts update Inventory through transactions, ensuring stock accuracy.
- Vendor records are shared across procurement and parts catalogs.

Key models: purchase.order.model.js, replenish.order.model.js, vendor.model.js, document.file.model.js


Tagging & Search

Make everything findable and consistently classified. Tags are curated and color-coded, with inheritance rules so standards apply everywhere (for example, templates pushing default tags to forms where they appear as non-removable inherited tags). Global search supports partial matching across critical fields and respects saved searches and user-friendly controls.

What it does for you:
- Quickly locates vessels, components, activities, documents, forms, and history.
- Keeps classification consistent across modules with minimal user effort.
- Supports saved searches and targeted filtering with chip-based controls.

Important behaviors and relationships:
- Tag selector uses an existing tag catalog (no ad-hoc tag creation at the point of use).
- Tag colors improve readability; inheritance gives consistent tagging without manual repetition.
- Tags are shared across maintenance, forms, documents, and can be used in global search filters.

Key models: searchable.tag.model.js, global.search.model.js"
    }
  }
]
